003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.utils;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.SystemUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
022
023import java.io.*;
024import java.nio.charset.*;
025import java.nio.file.*;
026import java.util.*;
027import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
028import java.util.function.*;
029
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.io.*;
031
032/**
033 * Various I/O related utility methods.
034 */
035public class IoUtils {
036
037   /** UTF-8 charset */
038   public static final Charset UTF8 = StandardCharsets.UTF_8;
039
040   /** Reusable empty input stream. */
041   @SuppressWarnings({
042      "resource" // Intentional singleton; read() always returns -1 and close() is a no-op
043   })
044   public static final InputStream EMPTY_INPUT_STREAM = new InputStream() {
045      @Override
046      public int read() {
047         return -1;  // end of stream
048      }
049   };
050
051   private static final int BUFF_SIZE = 1024;
052
053   @SuppressWarnings({
054      "java:S5164" // Cleanup method provided: cleanupThreadLocals()
055   })
056   private static final ThreadLocal<byte[]> BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE = (Boolean.TRUE.equals(env("juneau.disableIoBufferReuse", false)) ? null : new ThreadLocal<>());
057   @SuppressWarnings({
058      "java:S5164" // Cleanup method provided: cleanupThreadLocals()
059   })
060   private static final ThreadLocal<char[]> CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE = (Boolean.TRUE.equals(env("juneau.disableIoBufferReuse", false)) ? null : new ThreadLocal<>());
061   static final AtomicInteger BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE_HITS = new AtomicInteger();
062
063   static final AtomicInteger BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE_MISSES = new AtomicInteger();
064   static final AtomicInteger CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE_HITS = new AtomicInteger();
065   static final AtomicInteger CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE_MISSES = new AtomicInteger();
066   static {
067      shutdownMessage(() -> "Byte buffer cache:  hits=" + BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE_HITS.get() + ", misses=" + BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE_MISSES);
068      shutdownMessage(() -> "Char buffer cache:  hits=" + CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE_HITS.get() + ", misses=" + CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE_MISSES);
069   }
070
071   /**
072    * Cleans up thread-local buffer caches for the current thread.
073    *
074    * <p>
075    * This method should be called when a thread is finished using the buffer caches to prevent memory leaks
076    * in thread pool environments where threads are reused.
077    */
078   public static void cleanupThreadLocals() {
079      if (BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE != null) {
080         BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE.remove();
081      }
082      if (CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE != null) {
083         CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE.remove();
084      }
085   }
086
087   /** Reusable empty reader. */
088   @SuppressWarnings({
089      "resource" // Intentional singleton; read() always returns -1 and close() is a no-op
090   })
091   public static final Reader EMPTY_READER = new Reader() {
092      @Override
093      public void close() throws IOException { /* no-op */ }
094
095      @Override
096      public int read() {
097         return -1;  // end of stream
098      }
099
100      @Override
101      public int read(char[] cbuf, int off, int len) throws IOException {
102         return -1;  // end of stream
103      }
104   };
105
106   /**
107    * Close all specified input streams, output streams, readers, and writers.
108    *
109    * @param o
110    *    The list of all objects to close.
111    *    <jk>null</jk> entries are ignored.
112    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
113    */
114   @SuppressWarnings({
115      "null" // Null analysis not applicable to variadic close operations
116   })
117   public static void close(Object...o) throws IOException {
118      IOException ex = null;
119      for (var o2 : o) {
120         try {
121            if (o2 instanceof InputStream o3)
122               o3.close();
123            if (o2 instanceof OutputStream o3)
124               o3.close();
125            if (o2 instanceof Reader o3)
126               o3.close();
127            if (o2 instanceof Writer o3)
128               o3.close();
129         } catch (IOException e) {
130            ex = e;
131         }
132      }
133      if (nn(ex))
134         throw ex;
135   }
136
137   /**
138    * Close input stream and ignore any exceptions.
139    *
140    * <p>
141    * No-op if input stream is <jk>null</jk>.
142    *
143    * @param is The input stream to close.
144    */
145   public static void closeQuietly(InputStream is) {
146      if (nn(is))
147         safe(is::close);
148   }
149
150   /**
151    * Quietly close all specified input streams, output streams, readers, and writers.
152    *
153    * @param o The list of all objects to quietly close.
154    */
155   public static void closeQuietly(Object...o) {
156      for (var o2 : o) {
157         if (o2 instanceof InputStream o3)
158            closeQuietly(o3);
159         if (o2 instanceof OutputStream o3)
160            closeQuietly(o3);
161         if (o2 instanceof Reader o3)
162            closeQuietly(o3);
163         if (o2 instanceof Writer o3)
164            closeQuietly(o3);
165      }
166   }
167
168   /**
169    * Close output stream and ignore any exceptions.
170    *
171    * <p>
172    * No-op if output stream is <jk>null</jk>.
173    *
174    * @param os The output stream to close.
175    */
176   public static void closeQuietly(OutputStream os) {
177      if (nn(os))
178         quiet(os::close);
179   }
180
181   /**
182    * Close reader and ignore any exceptions.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * No-op if reader is <jk>null</jk>.
186    *
187    * @param r The reader to close.
188    */
189   public static void closeQuietly(Reader r) {
190      if (nn(r))
191         quiet(r::close);
192   }
193
194   /**
195    * Close writer and ignore any exceptions.
196    *
197    * <p>
198    * No-op if writer is <jk>null</jk>.
199    *
200    * @param w The writer to close.
201    */
202   public static void closeQuietly(Writer w) {
203      if (nn(w))
204         quiet(w::close);
205   }
206
207   /**
208    * Counts the number of bytes in the input stream and then closes the stream.
209    *
210    * @param is The input stream to read from.
211    * @return The number of bytes read.
212    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
213    */
214   public static long count(InputStream is) throws IOException {
215      if (is == null)
216         return 0;
217      var c = 0l;
218      var i = 0l;
219      try {
220         while ((i = is.skip(1024)) != 0)
221            c += i;
222      } finally {
223         is.close();
224      }
225      return c;
226   }
227
228   /**
229    * Counts the number of characters in the reader and then closes the reader.
230    *
231    * @param r The reader to read from.
232    * @return The number of characters read.
233    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
234    */
235   public static long count(Reader r) throws IOException {
236      if (r == null)
237         return 0;
238      var c = 0l;
239      var i = 0l;
240      try {
241         while ((i = r.skip(1024)) != 0)
242            c += i;
243      } finally {
244         r.close();
245      }
246      return c;
247   }
248
249   /**
250    * Flushes multiple output streams and writers in a single call.
251    *
252    * @param o
253    *    The objects to flush.
254    *    <jk>null</jk> entries are ignored.
255    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
256    */
257   @SuppressWarnings({
258      "null" // Null analysis not applicable to variadic flush operations
259   })
260   public static void flush(Object...o) throws IOException {
261      IOException ex = null;
262      for (var o2 : o) {
263         try {
264            if (o2 instanceof OutputStream o3)
265               o3.flush();
266            if (o2 instanceof Writer o3)
267               o3.flush();
268         } catch (IOException e) {
269            ex = e;
270         }
271      }
272      if (nn(ex))
273         throw ex;
274   }
275
276   /**
277    * Loads a text file from either the file system or classpath.
278    *
279    * @param name The file name.
280    * @param paths The paths to search.
281    * @return The file contents, or <jk>null</jk> if not found.
282    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
283    */
284   public static String loadSystemResourceAsString(String name, String...paths) throws IOException {
285      for (var path : paths) {
286         var p = new File(path);
287         if (p.exists()) {
288            var f = new File(p, name);
289            if (f.exists() && f.canRead())
290               return read(f);
291         }
292      }
293      var cl = Thread.currentThread().getContextClassLoader();
294      if (cl == null)
295         cl = ClassLoader.getSystemClassLoader();
296      for (var path : paths) {
297         var n = ".".equals(path) ? name : path + '/' + name;
298         try (var is = cl.getResourceAsStream(n)) {
299            if (nn(is))
300               return read(is);
301         }
302         try (var is = ClassLoader.getSystemResourceAsStream(n)) {
303            if (nn(is))
304               return read(is);
305         }
306      }
307      return null;
308   }
309
310   /**
311    * Pipes the specified byte array to the specified output stream.
312    *
313    * @param in
314    *    The input byte array.
315    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
316    * @param out
317    *    The output stream.
318    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
319    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
320    * @param maxBytes
321    *    The maximum number of bytes or <c>-1</c> to read the entire byte array.
322    * @return The number of bytes written.
323    * @throws IOException If thrown from output stream.
324    */
325   public static long pipe(byte[] in, OutputStream out, int maxBytes) throws IOException {
326      if (in == null || out == null)
327         return 0;
328      var length = (maxBytes < 0 || maxBytes > in.length) ? in.length : maxBytes;
329      out.write(in, 0, length);
330      return length;
331   }
332
333   /**
334    * Pipes the specified input stream to the specified output stream.
335    *
336    * <p>
337    * Either stream is not automatically closed.
338    *
339    * @param in
340    *    The input stream.
341    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
342    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
343    * @param out
344    *    The output stream.
345    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
346    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
347    * @return The number of bytes written.
348    * @throws IOException If thrown from either stream.
349    */
350   public static long pipe(InputStream in, OutputStream out) throws IOException {
351      try (var in2 = in) {
352         return pipe(in, out, -1);
353      }
354   }
355
356   /**
357    * Pipes the specified input stream to the specified output stream.
358    *
359    * <p>
360    * Either stream is not automatically closed.
361    *
362    * @param in
363    *    The input stream.
364    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
365    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
366    * @param out
367    *    The output stream.
368    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
369    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
370    * @param onException Consumer of any {@link IOException I/O exceptions}.
371    * @return The number of bytes written.
372    */
373   public static long pipe(InputStream in, OutputStream out, Consumer<IOException> onException) {
374      try {
375         try (var in2 = in) {
376            return pipe(in, out, -1);
377         }
378      } catch (IOException e) {
379         onException.accept(e);
380         return -1;
381      }
382   }
383
384   /**
385    * Pipes the specified input stream to the specified output stream.
386    *
387    * <p>
388    * Either stream is not automatically closed.
389    *
390    * @param in
391    *    The input stream.
392    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
393    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
394    * @param out
395    *    The output stream.
396    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
397    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
398    * @param maxBytes
399    *    The maximum number of bytes or <c>-1</c> to read the entire input stream.
400    * @return The number of bytes written.
401    * @throws IOException If thrown from either stream.
402    */
403   public static long pipe(InputStream in, OutputStream out, long maxBytes) throws IOException {
404      if (in == null || out == null)
405         return 0;
406      var buffer = byteBuffer((int)maxBytes);
407      int readLen;
408      var total = 0l;
409      if (maxBytes < 0) {
410         while ((readLen = in.read(buffer)) != -1) {
411            out.write(buffer, 0, readLen);
412            total += readLen;
413         }
414      } else {
415         var remaining = maxBytes;
416         while (remaining > 0) {
417            readLen = in.read(buffer, 0, buffSize(remaining));
418            if (readLen == -1)
419               break;
420            out.write(buffer, 0, readLen);
421            total += readLen;
422            remaining -= readLen;
423         }
424      }
425      out.flush();
426      return total;
427   }
428
429   /**
430    * Pipes the contents of the specified input stream to the writer.
431    *
432    * @param in
433    *    The stream to pipe from.
434    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
435    *    <br>Streams is automatically closed.
436    * @param out
437    *    The writer to pipe to.
438    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
439    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
440    * @return
441    *    The number of bytes written.
442    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
443    */
444   public static long pipe(InputStream in, Writer out) throws IOException {
445      if (in == null || out == null)
446         return 0;
447      return pipe(new InputStreamReader(in, UTF8), out);
448   }
449
450   /**
451    * Pipes the contents of the specified input stream to the writer.
452    *
453    * @param in
454    *    The stream to pipe from.
455    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
456    *    <br>Streams is automatically closed.
457    * @param out
458    *    The writer to pipe to.
459    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
460    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
461    * @param onException Consumer of any {@link IOException I/O exceptions}.
462    * @return
463    *    The number of bytes written.
464    */
465   public static long pipe(InputStream in, Writer out, Consumer<IOException> onException) {
466      try {
467         if (in == null || out == null)
468            return 0;
469         return pipe(new InputStreamReader(in, UTF8), out);
470      } catch (IOException e) {
471         onException.accept(e);
472         return -2;
473      }
474   }
475
476   /**
477    * Pipes the contents of the specified <c>Reader</c> to the specified file.
478    *
479    * @param in
480    *    The reader to pipe from.
481    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
482    *    <br>Reader is automatically closed.
483    * @param out
484    *    The file to write the output to.
485    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
486    * @return
487    *    The number of characters piped.
488    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
489    */
490   public static long pipe(Reader in, File out) throws IOException {
491      if (out == null || in == null)
492         return 0;
493      try (var w = FileWriterBuilder.create(out).buffered().build()) {
494         return pipe(in, w);
495      }
496   }
497
498   /**
499    * Pipes the specified reader to the specified output stream.
500    *
501    * @param in
502    *    The input reader.
503    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
504    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
505    * @param out
506    *    The output stream.
507    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
508    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
509    * @return The number of bytes written.
510    * @throws IOException If thrown from output stream.
511    */
512   public static long pipe(Reader in, OutputStream out) throws IOException {
513      if (in == null || out == null)
514         return 0;
515      var total = 0l;
516      try (var in2 = in) {
517         var osw = new OutputStreamWriter(out, UTF8);
518         var i = 0;
519         var b = charBuffer(-1);
520         while ((i = in.read(b)) > 0) {
521            total += i;
522            osw.write(b, 0, i);
523         }
524         osw.flush();
525      }
526      return total;
527   }
528
529   /**
530    * Pipes the specified reader to the specified output stream.
531    *
532    * @param in
533    *    The input reader.
534    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
535    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
536    * @param out
537    *    The output stream.
538    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
539    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
540    * @param onException Consumer of any {@link IOException I/O exceptions}.
541    * @return The number of bytes written.
542    */
543   public static long pipe(Reader in, OutputStream out, Consumer<IOException> onException) {
544      try {
545         return pipe(in, out);
546      } catch (IOException e) {
547         onException.accept(e);
548         return -1;
549      }
550   }
551
552   /**
553    * Pipes the contents of the specified <c>Reader</c> to the specified <c>Writer</c>.
554    *
555    * @param in
556    *    The reader to pipe from.
557    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
558    *    <br>Reader is automatically closed.
559    * @param out
560    *    The file to write the output to.
561    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
562    *    <br>Writer is flushed but not automatically closed.
563    * @return
564    *    The number of characters piped.
565    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
566    */
567   public static long pipe(Reader in, Writer out) throws IOException {
568      if (out == null || in == null)
569         return 0;
570      var total = 0l;
571      try (var in2 = in) {
572         var buffer = charBuffer(-1);
573         int readLen;
574         while ((readLen = in.read(buffer)) != -1) {
575            out.write(buffer, 0, readLen);
576            total += readLen;
577         }
578      }
579      out.flush();
580      return total;
581   }
582
583   /**
584    * Pipes the contents of the specified <c>Reader</c> to the specified <c>Writer</c>.
585    *
586    * @param in
587    *    The reader to pipe from.
588    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
589    *    <br>Reader is automatically closed.
590    * @param out
591    *    The file to write the output to.
592    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
593    *    <br>Writer is flushed but not automatically closed.
594    * @param onException Consumer of any {@link IOException I/O exceptions}.
595    * @return
596    *    The number of characters piped.
597    */
598   public static long pipe(Reader in, Writer out, Consumer<IOException> onException) {
599      try {
600         return pipe(in, out);
601      } catch (IOException e) {
602         onException.accept(e);
603         return -1;
604      }
605   }
606
607   /**
608    * Pipes the contents of the specified <c>Reader</c> to the specified <c>Writer</c> a line at a time.
609    *
610    * <p>
611    * Writer is flushed after every line.  Typically useful when writing to consoles.
612    *
613    * @param in
614    *    The reader to pipe from.
615    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
616    *    <br>Reader is automatically closed.
617    * @param out
618    *    The file to write the output to.
619    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
620    *    <br>Writer is flushed but not automatically closed.
621    * @return
622    *    The number of characters piped.
623    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
624    */
625   public static long pipeLines(Reader in, Writer out) throws IOException {
626      if (in == null || out == null)
627         return 0;
628      var total = 0l;
629      try (var in2 = in) {
630         try (var s = new Scanner(in2)) {
631            while (s.hasNextLine()) {
632               var l = s.nextLine();
633               if (nn(l)) {
634                  out.write(l);
635                  out.write("\n");
636                  out.flush();
637                  total += l.length() + 1;
638               }
639            }
640         }
641      }
642      return total;
643   }
644
645   /**
646    * Reads the specified byte array containing UTF-8 into a string.
647    *
648    * @param in
649    *    The input.
650    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
651    * @return The new string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was null.
652    */
653   public static String read(byte[] in) {
654      return read(in, UTF8);
655   }
656
657   /**
658    * Reads the specified byte array into a string.
659    *
660    * @param in
661    *    The input.
662    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
663    * @param charset The character set to use for decoding.
664    * @return The new string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was null.
665    */
666   public static String read(byte[] in, Charset charset) {
667      if (in == null)
668         return null;
669      return new String(in, charset);
670   }
671
672   /**
673    * Reads the contents of a file into a string.
674    *
675    * <p>
676    * Assumes default character encoding.
677    *
678    * @param in
679    *  The file to read.
680    *  <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
681    * @return
682    *  The contents of the reader as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if file does not exist.
683    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
684    */
685   public static String read(File in) throws IOException {
686      if (in == null || ! in.exists())
687         return null;
688      try (var r = FileReaderBuilder.create(in).build()) {
689         return read(r, in.length());
690      }
691   }
692
693   /**
694    * Reads the contents of an input stream into a string.
695    *
696    * <p>
697    * Assumes UTF-8 encoding.
698    *
699    * @param in
700    *    The input stream.
701    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
702    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
703    * @return
704    *    The contents of the reader as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input stream was <jk>null</jk>.
705    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the input stream.
706    */
707   public static String read(InputStream in) throws IOException {
708      return read(in, UTF8);
709   }
710
711   /**
712    * Reads the contents of an input stream into a string using the specified charset.
713    *
714    * @param in
715    *    The input stream.
716    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
717    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
718    * @param cs
719    *    The charset of the contents of the input stream.
720    * @return
721    *    The contents of the reader as a string or <jk>null</jk> if input stream was <jk>null</jk>.
722    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the input stream.
723    */
724   public static String read(InputStream in, Charset cs) throws IOException {
725      if (in == null)
726         return null;
727      try (var isr = new InputStreamReader(in, cs)) {
728         return read(isr);
729      }
730   }
731
732   /**
733    * Reads the contents of an input stream into a string using the specified charset.
734    *
735    * @param in
736    *    The input stream.
737    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
738    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
739    * @param cs
740    *    The charset of the contents of the input stream.
741    * @param onException Consumer of any {@link IOException I/O exceptions}.
742    * @return
743    *    The contents of the reader as a string or <jk>null</jk> if input stream was <jk>null</jk>.
744    */
745   public static String read(InputStream in, Charset cs, Consumer<IOException> onException) {
746      if (in == null)
747         return null;
748      try (var isr = new InputStreamReader(in, cs)) {
749         return read(isr);
750      } catch (IOException e) {
751         onException.accept(e);
752         return null;
753      }
754   }
755
756   /**
757    * Reads the contents of an input stream into a string.
758    *
759    * <p>
760    * Assumes UTF-8 encoding.
761    *
762    * @param in
763    *    The input stream.
764    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
765    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
766    * @param onException Consumer of any {@link IOException I/O exceptions}.
767    * @return
768    *    The contents of the reader as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input stream was <jk>null</jk>.
769    */
770   public static String read(InputStream in, Consumer<IOException> onException) {
771      return read(in, UTF8, onException);
772   }
773
774   /**
775    * Reads the contents of an input stream into a string, reading up to the specified maximum number of bytes.
776    *
777    * <p>
778    * Assumes UTF-8 encoding.
779    *
780    * @param in
781    *    The input stream.
782    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
783    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
784    * @param maxBytes
785    *    The maximum number of bytes to read, or <c>-1</c> to read all bytes.
786    * @return
787    *    The contents of the input stream as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input stream was <jk>null</jk>.
788    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the input stream.
789    */
790   public static String read(InputStream in, int maxBytes) throws IOException {
791      return read(in, maxBytes, UTF8);
792   }
793
794   /**
795    * Reads the contents of an input stream into a string using the specified charset, reading up to the specified maximum number of bytes.
796    *
797    * @param in
798    *    The input stream.
799    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
800    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
801    * @param maxBytes
802    *    The maximum number of bytes to read, or <c>-1</c> to read all bytes.
803    * @param cs
804    *    The charset of the contents of the input stream.
805    * @return
806    *    The contents of the input stream as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input stream was <jk>null</jk>.
807    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the input stream.
808    */
809   public static String read(InputStream in, int maxBytes, Charset cs) throws IOException {
810      if (in == null)
811         return null;
812      try (var in2 = in) {
813         var bytes = readBytes(in2, maxBytes);
814         return new String(bytes, cs);
815      }
816   }
817
818   /**
819    * Reads the contents of an input stream into a string, reading up to the specified maximum number of bytes.
820    *
821    * <p>
822    * Assumes UTF-8 encoding.
823    *
824    * @param in
825    *    The input stream.
826    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
827    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
828    * @param maxBytes
829    *    The maximum number of bytes to read, or <c>-1</c> to read all bytes.
830    * @return
831    *    The contents of the input stream as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input stream was <jk>null</jk>.
832    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the input stream.
833    */
834   public static String read(InputStream in, long maxBytes) throws IOException {
835      if (maxBytes > Integer.MAX_VALUE)
836         maxBytes = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
837      return read(in, (int)maxBytes, UTF8);
838   }
839
840   /**
841    * Pipes the specified object to the specified output stream.
842    *
843    * @param in
844    *    The input byte array.
845    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> or any of the following types:
846    *    <ul>
847    *       <li>{@link Reader}
848    *       <li>{@link InputStream}
849    *       <li>{@link File}
850    *       <li>byte array.
851    *    </ul>
852    * @return The input converted to a string.
853    * @throws IOException If thrown from output stream.
854    */
855   public static String read(Object in) throws IOException {
856      if (in == null)
857         return null;
858      if (in instanceof Reader in2)
859         return read(in2);
860      if (in instanceof InputStream in2)
861         return read(in2);
862      if (in instanceof File in2)
863         return read(in2);
864      if (in instanceof byte[] in2)
865         return read(in2);
866      throw illegalArg("Invalid type passed to read:  {0}", cn(in));
867   }
868
869   /**
870    * Reads the contents of a path into a string.
871    *
872    * <p>
873    * Assumes default character encoding.
874    *
875    * @param in
876    *  The path to read.
877    *  <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
878    * @return
879    *  The contents of the reader as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if path does not exist.
880    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
881    * @since 9.1.0
882    */
883   public static String read(Path in) throws IOException {
884      if (in == null || ! Files.exists(in)) {
885         return null;
886      }
887      try (var r = PathReaderBuilder.create(in).build()) {
888         return read(r, Files.size(in));
889      }
890   }
891
892   /**
893    * Reads the contents of a reader into a string.
894    *
895    * @param in
896    *    The input reader.
897    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
898    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
899    * @return
900    *    The contents of the reader as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if the reader was <jk>null</jk>.
901    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
902    */
903   public static String read(Reader in) throws IOException {
904      try (var in2 = in) {
905         return read(in, -1);
906      }
907   }
908
909   /**
910    * Reads the contents of a reader into a string.
911    *
912    * @param in
913    *    The input reader.
914    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
915    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
916    * @param onException Consumer of any {@link IOException I/O exceptions}.
917    * @return
918    *    The contents of the reader as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if the reader was <jk>null</jk>.
919    */
920   public static String read(Reader in, Consumer<IOException> onException) {
921      try (var in2 = in) {
922         return read(in, -1);
923      } catch (IOException e) {
924         onException.accept(e);
925         return null;
926      }
927   }
928
929   /**
930    * Reads the specified input into a {@link String} until the end of the input is reached.
931    *
932    * @param in
933    *    The input reader.
934    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
935    *    <br>String is automatically closed.
936    * @param expectedLength
937    *    Specify a positive number if the length of the input is known, or <c>-1</c> if unknown.
938    * @return
939    *    The contents of the reader as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if the reader was <jk>null</jk>.
940    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
941    */
942   public static String read(Reader in, long expectedLength) throws IOException {
943      if (in == null)
944         return null;
945      try (var in2 = in) {
946         var sb = new StringBuilder(buffSize(expectedLength)); // Assume they're ASCII characters.
947         var buf = charBuffer((int)expectedLength);
948         var i = 0;
949         while ((i = in2.read(buf)) != -1)
950            sb.append(buf, 0, i);
951         return sb.toString();
952      }
953   }
954
955   /**
956    * Read the specified file into a byte array.
957    *
958    * @param in
959    *    The file to read into a byte array.
960    * @return The contents of the file as a byte array.
961    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
962    */
963   public static byte[] readBytes(File in) throws IOException {
964      return readBytes(in, -1);
965   }
966
967   /**
968    * Read the specified file into a byte array.
969    *
970    * @param in
971    *    The file to read into a byte array.
972    * @param maxBytes
973    *    The maximum number of bytes to read, or <jk>-1</jk> to read all bytes.
974    * @return The contents of the file as a byte array.
975    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
976    */
977   public static byte[] readBytes(File in, int maxBytes) throws IOException {
978      if (in == null || ! (in.exists() && in.canRead()))
979         return new byte[0];
980      try (var is = new FileInputStream(in)) {
981         return readBytes(is, maxBytes);
982      }
983   }
984
985   /**
986    * Reads the specified input stream into the specified byte array.
987    *
988    * @param in
989    *    The input stream to read.
990    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
991    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
992    * @return A byte array containing the contents.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
993    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
994    */
995   public static byte[] readBytes(InputStream in) throws IOException {
996      try (var in2 = in) {
997         return readBytes(in2, -1);
998      }
999   }
1000
1001   /**
1002    * Reads the specified input stream into the specified byte array.
1003    *
1004    * @param in
1005    *    The input stream to read.
1006    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1007    *    <br>Stream is not automatically closed.
1008    * @param maxBytes
1009    *    The maximum number of bytes or <c>-1</c> to read the entire stream.
1010    * @return A byte array containing the contents.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1011    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1012    */
1013   public static byte[] readBytes(InputStream in, int maxBytes) throws IOException {
1014      if (in == null)
1015         return new byte[0];
1016      var buff = new ByteArrayOutputStream(buffSize(maxBytes));
1017      var nRead = 0;
1018      var b = byteBuffer(maxBytes);
1019      while ((nRead = in.read(b, 0, b.length)) != -1)
1020         buff.write(b, 0, nRead);
1021      buff.flush();
1022      return buff.toByteArray();
1023   }
1024
1025   /**
1026    * Reads the specified input stream into the specified byte array.
1027    *
1028    * @param in
1029    *    The input stream to read.
1030    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1031    *    <br>Stream is automatically closed.
1032    * @return A byte array containing the contents.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1033    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1034    */
1035   public static byte[] readBytes(Reader in) throws IOException {
1036      if (in == null)
1037         return new byte[0];
1038      try (var in2 = in) {
1039         return read(in2, -1).getBytes();
1040      }
1041   }
1042
1043   /**
1044    * Wraps the specified reader in a buffered reader.
1045    *
1046    * @param r The reader being wrapped.
1047    * @return
1048    *    The reader wrapped in a {@link BufferedReader}, or the original {@link Reader} if it's already a buffered
1049    *    reader.
1050    */
1051   @SuppressWarnings({
1052      "resource" // Caller takes ownership of the returned Reader
1053   })
1054   public static Reader toBufferedReader(Reader r) {
1055      if (r == null || r instanceof BufferedReader || r instanceof StringReader)
1056         return r;
1057      return new BufferedReader(r);
1058   }
1059
1060   private static int buffSize(long max) {
1061      return (max > 0 && max < BUFF_SIZE) ? (int)max : BUFF_SIZE;
1062   }
1063
1064   private static byte[] byteBuffer(int maxBytes) {
1065      if (nn(BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE)) {
1066         var x = BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE.get();
1067         if (x == null) {
1068            x = new byte[BUFF_SIZE];
1069            BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE.set(x);
1070            BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE_MISSES.incrementAndGet();
1071         } else {
1072            BYTE_BUFFER_CACHE_HITS.incrementAndGet();
1073         }
1074         return x;
1075      }
1076      return new byte[buffSize(maxBytes)];
1077   }
1078
1079   private static char[] charBuffer(int maxChars) {
1080      if (nn(CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE)) {
1081         var x = CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE.get();
1082         if (x == null) {
1083            x = new char[BUFF_SIZE];
1084            CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE.set(x);
1085            CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE_MISSES.incrementAndGet();
1086         } else {
1087            CHAR_BUFFER_CACHE_HITS.incrementAndGet();
1088         }
1089         return x;
1090      }
1091      return new char[buffSize(maxChars)];
1092   }
1093
1094   /**
1095    * Constructor.
1096    */
1097   protected IoUtils() {}
1098}
